answers.value: From Columbia, take Hwy 63 north approximately 9 miles to Route HH. Turn left (west) on HH and continue for 6.2 miles until the pavement ends. Turn right onto Prairie Hollow Road (gravel). Follow Prairie Hollow Road for 1.4 miles; the Whitaker Farm driveway is on the left, marked by a green mailbox with the number 7423 and a red barn-shaped sign that reads "Whitaker Farm - Game Birds". Drive up the gravel lane past the white farmhouse; the flight pens and brooder house are located behind the large red equipment shed on the south side of the property.
answers.reasoning: Pre-filled value from ground truth form

answers.field_label: Species To Be Covered By Permit
answers.value: Ring-necked pheasant (Phasianus colchicus) and Northern bobwhite quail (Colinus virginianus). I maintain small hobby flocks of both species for personal enjoyment, dog training, and educational demonstrations for local 4-H youth. At any given time, I expect to have no more than 75 adult pheasants and 150 adult quail on the property, plus seasonal chicks during the spring and early summer hatching period. Birds are kept in secure outdoor flight pens with covered tops to prevent escape and to protect from predators, as well as in enclosed brooder facilities for young chicks. I do not keep any other wildlife species under this permit and have no plans to add additional species beyond these two upland game birds.
answers.reasoning: Pre-filled value from ground truth form
